global_cues.us_markets: S&P 500 +0.12% to 7,173.91 (record close), Nasdaq +0.2% to 24,887.10 (record close), Dow -0.13% to 49,167.79. Records limited by oil spike on stalled US-Iran talks.
global_cues.european_markets: Mixed close. CAC flat at 8,158 (+0.01%), DAX +0.2% to 24,194, FTSE -0.3% to 10,341. Investors cautious ahead of central bank meetings; oil rising on Iran tensions.
global_cues.asian_markets: GIFT Nifty trading near 24,105 (-0.05% from prior close 24,116, range 23,984-24,146), pointing to a flat-to-mildly-negative open. Asia-Pacific broadly higher in prior session despite Iran setbacks.

fii_dii_summary: Apr 27: FII net sellers ₹1,151.48 cr in cash; DII net buyers ₹4,123.92 cr. DII absorption continues to offset persistent FII outflows — domestic liquidity remains the key support.
earnings_calendar: Maruti Suzuki - Apr 28 (board meet, Q4 FY26; consensus expects ~27% YoY revenue growth)
Nippon Life India Asset Management - Apr 28
SBI Cards and Payment Services - Apr 28
macro_events: India IIP (Industrial Production) data for March 2026 releases today (Apr 28)
RBI policy rates held: Repo 5.25%, SDF 5.00%, MSF/Bank Rate 5.50%, CRR 0%, SLR 18%
India CPI inflation at 3.4% in March 2026 (vs 3.21% in Feb), within RBI's 2-6% band
FY27 RBI projections: GDP +6.9%, CPI 4.6%, core inflation 4.4%
New inflation-targeting framework notified: 4% target with 2-6% band for Apr 2026-Mar 2031
India forex reserves data due May 1
RBI April 2026 monthly bulletin published Apr 24
sector_themes: Pharma outperforming: Sun Pharma +7% on Organon acquisition; Nifty Pharma sector strong
IT mixed: sector bounced but weak revenue guidance from majors keeps analysts cautious on fresh long entries
Banking: Nifty Private Bank underperforming; Bank Nifty at 56,264; PSU/SFB earnings today (AU SFB, Punjab & Sind, SBI Cards) in focus
Auto: Positive momentum into Maruti Suzuki's Q4 print; FY26 production hit record 2.34m units
Metals participating in broad-based buying alongside pharma/IT/auto
Energy/Oil & Gas: Adani Total Gas earnings today amid elevated crude backdrop
risk_flags: Crude oil elevated: WTI ~$96.39 (+2.11% Apr 27), Brent steady near $105 — IEA warning of unprecedented supply shock
US-Iran peace talks stalled; fresh escalation in Strait of Hormuz; West Asia conflict in 9th week
USD/INR at ₹94.20 (range ₹92-94); RBI intervention providing floor — imported inflation risk if rupee slides
RBI flagged supply-side inflation risks from West Asia conflict and weather uncertainty
Persistent FII selling (cumulative outflows pressuring INR and large-cap valuations)
Crowded earnings week — single-stock volatility risk elevated
brief_summary: Indian markets head into Apr 28 with mixed global cues — US S&P 500 and Nasdaq printed fresh record closes but Dow slipped, while GIFT Nifty signals a flat open. Crude near $96-105 on stalled US-Iran talks and continued FII selling (₹1,151 cr) are headwinds, but heavy DII buying (₹4,124 cr) and a packed earnings docket headlined by Maruti Suzuki plus March IIP data should drive stock-specific action over index-level moves. Bias: cautiously constructive on pharma/auto, selective on IT, neutral on banks pending earnings.
